deep_dive.title: Environment-free Synthetic Data Generation for API-Calling Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Training API-calling large language model (LLM) agents demands massive amounts of high-quality trajectories. However, collecting such data at scale typically requires fully implemented environments with executable APIs and realistic, pre-populated backend databases, creating a major bottleneck for scalability. To overcome this, we propose an environment-free synthetic data generation approach that leverages LLMs as on-the-fly digital world models. Given only API specifications, our method generates trajectories mimicking interactions between an agent and a stateful environment. Specifically, an LLM first generates diverse tasks solvable with the provided APIs. A teacher agent then iteratively solves each task while an LLM simulator generates coherent synthetic API responses conditioned on the task context and simulation history. Finally, an LLM judge filters the trajectories to ensure the quality of the resulting dataset. We evaluate our approach on the challenging AppWorld and OfficeBench benchmarks, which include both information-retrieval and state-changing tasks. Fine-tuning models on our synthetic data yields significant performance gains, demonstrating that effective supervision for API-calling agents can be generated without any executable environment. Our results establish LLM-based API simulation as a practical, scalable solution for training agents across diverse API ecosystems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: synthetic data generation for training API-calling agents without environment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需环境为API调用代理生成合成训练数据
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需环境为API调用代理生成合成训练数据
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无需环境为API调用代理生成合成训练数据
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes an environment-free synthetic data generation approach that uses LLMs as world models to create training trajectories for API-calling agents, addressing data scarcity and enabling scalable agent post-training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对API调用代理训练数据稀缺问题，提出无需真实环境的合成数据生成方法。利用大语言模型作为世界模型，仅凭API规范生成多样任务和教师代理求解轨迹，实现可扩展的代理后训练。该方法无需构建完整环境与后端数据库，显著降低了代理训练的前期成本，使得金融等领域的代理后训练更为便捷。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对API调用代理训练数据稀缺问题，提出无需真实环境的合成数据生成方法。利用大语言模型作为世界模型，仅凭API规范生成多样任务和教师代理求解轨迹，实现可扩展的代理后训练。该方法无需构建完整环境与后端数据库，显著降低了代理训练的前期成本，使得金融等领域的代理后训练更为便捷。

deep_dive.title: TriAgent: Divergence-Aware Multi-Agent Committees for Cost-Efficient Financial Sentiment Analysis
deep_dive.abstract: Production LLM-based financial sentiment analysis faces a structural cost trap: most queries are trivially classifiable, yet expensive cloud reasoners process them all, and the bill scales linearly with user count. We present TriAgent, a multi-agent committee stratified by contextual granularity -- a word-level lexicon (VADER), a sentence-level domain transformer (FinBERT), and a cross-sentence reasoner (Qwen2.5, 0.5B-14B-4bit, with Mistral-7B and Phi-3.5-mini cross-family checks). A three-way Semantic Divergence Index (SDI) measures pairwise disagreement across granularities and routes each query accordingly. Our central finding is the critic plateau: when the LLM is re-tasked as a critic over the smaller agents' outputs, F1 plateaus at ~0.87 across 1.5B-7B Qwen (bootstrap 95% CIs overlap), while a same-size 3-persona vote drops to F1=0.66, which is driven by granularity-stratified diversity. Three corollaries follow from the same SDI signal: (i) a Shared Consensus Dictionary on multilingual sentence-BERT answers 95% of Chinese queries from an English cache at F1=0.99 -- cross-border canonicalization at zero marginal cost; (ii) SDI doubles as a post-hoc LLM-hallucination detector at AUC=0.90; (iii) the SDI single-stage strategy attains the best risk-adjusted return (Sharpe=3.50) on a 20-ticker back-test, dominating both always-FinBERT (1.36) and always-LLM (0.11). At 10M-user scale, TriAgent saves $9.3M/year vs. a GPT-4o-mini baseline. Code, lexicons, and the SCD are released.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: TriAgent is a multi-agent committee for financial sentiment analysis, a task-oriented agent for finance
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: TriAgent是一个用于金融情感分析的多智能体委员会，属于面向金融领域的任务导向智能体
deep_dive.llm_evidence: TriAgent是一个用于金融情感分析的多智能体委员会，属于面向金融领域的任务导向智能体
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: TriAgent是一个用于金融情感分析的多智能体委员会，属于面向金融领域的任务导向智能体
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Divergence-aware multi-agent committee with VADER, FinBERT, and LLM reasoner routes financial sentiment queries cost-efficiently; LLM-as-critic achieves comparable accuracy at lower cost.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对金融情感分析中大型语言模型成本过高的问题，TriAgent提出了一种基于语义分歧的多智能体委员会，结合词汇级、句子级和跨句子模型进行分层路由，发现将LLM作为小模型输出的评判者时，既能保持高准确率又能显著降低成本。该工作为金融领域任务导向智能体提供了高效低成本的解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对金融情感分析中大型语言模型成本过高的问题，TriAgent提出了一种基于语义分歧的多智能体委员会，结合词汇级、句子级和跨句子模型进行分层路由，发现将LLM作为小模型输出的评判者时，既能保持高准确率又能显著降低成本。该工作为金融领域任务导向智能体提供了高效低成本的解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Co-Evolving LLM Evaluators and Policies via DynamicRubric
deep_dive.abstract: Post-training with evaluator feedback on policy-induced samples serves as a major mechanism for improving large language models. As policies improve, these sampled responses become close in quality. These close candidates create a bottleneck for policy optimization: collapsed relative evaluator score gaps yield weak or misleading policy supervision. We theoretically characterize why these gaps matter through a probability allocation view, showing that the directional gain of shifting probability mass from one response to another is exactly the evaluator score gap between them. This identifies relative score gaps as the policy optimization signals that guide updates. Motivated by this view, we propose DynamicRubric, a response-set-conditioned evaluator--policy co-evolution framework that generates weighted binary rubric items for each candidate set and aggregates the resulting judgments into response-level scores. In our experiments with 8B backbones, DynamicRubric improves evaluator performance and provides stronger policy supervision than baselines using a 70B reward model or a 235B static rubric generator. DynamicRubric-optimized policies also show gains on verifiable reasoning and coding tasks. A DynamicRubric-optimized model is fully deployed in WeChat Search's AI answering scenario, where it serves all online traffic across tens of millions of requests per day and improves key online metrics. These results suggest a principle for evaluator-guided post-training: evaluators should evolve with the policies they supervise.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes DynamicRubric, a co-evolving evaluator-policy framework for post-training LLM agents, addressing evaluator score collapse and policy optimization signals.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出DynamicRubric框架，通过评估器与策略协同进化提升LLM智能体后训练效果，解决评估分数崩塌和策略优化信号问题。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出DynamicRubric框架，通过评估器与策略协同进化提升LLM智能体后训练效果，解决评估分数崩塌和策略优化信号问题。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出DynamicRubric框架，通过评估器与策略协同进化提升LLM智能体后训练效果，解决评估分数崩塌和策略优化信号问题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Post-training LLMs with evaluator feedback suffers from collapsed score gaps when candidate responses are close. DynamicRubric conditions the evaluator on the response set, co-evolving it with the policy to maintain discriminative signals, improving policy optimization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 在后训练阶段，当LLM生成的候选回答质量相近时，评估器的打分差距缩小会导致策略优化信号减弱或误导。本文提出DynamicRubric方法，通过让评估器以响应集为条件并与策略协同进化，保持评估的区分性，从而改善策略优化。理论分析揭示了评估分数差距作为策略更新方向增益的关键作用。实验表明该方法能有效提升策略训练效果，为解决后训练中评估瓶颈提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 在后训练阶段，当LLM生成的候选回答质量相近时，评估器的打分差距缩小会导致策略优化信号减弱或误导。本文提出DynamicRubric方法，通过让评估器以响应集为条件并与策略协同进化，保持评估的区分性，从而改善策略优化。理论分析揭示了评估分数差距作为策略更新方向增益的关键作用。实验表明该方法能有效提升策略训练效果，为解决后训练中评估瓶颈提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Knowledge-Centric Self-Improvement
quick_skim.abstract: Self-improving AI systems typically treat the agent as the object that improves, by optimizing prompts, workflows, harnesses, or even the agent's own code. This agent-centric view can make improvements expensive to maintain and difficult to transfer, because gains become tied to a particular agent design, task distribution, or adaptation run. We study a complementary paradigm: knowledge-centric self-improvement, in which agents remain generic and disposable while the persistent object is a curated knowledge base that agents can leverage for future tasks. We conduct controlled case studies to operationalize this idea via a simple protocol. Agents attempt one task, then contribute evidence-grounded insights to a shared knowledge base via task-level and cross-task forums, followed by knowledge distillation. Because self-improvement is contained in the knowledge rather than the agent, improvement can be more inspectable, transferable, and portable. Across abstract reasoning, coding, and terminal benchmarks, this protocol improves solve rates while reducing dollar cost relative to agent-centric baselines. The resulting distilled knowledge also transfers to held-out tasks and across LLM families, indicating that the improvement is not merely an LLM- or run-specific behavior. These results support a new view of self-improving agentic systems: progress can be driven primarily by the curated persistent knowledge. Code is available at https://github.com/recursive-knowledge/KSI.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a knowledge-centric paradigm for agent self-improvement as a post-training technique
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出以知识为中心的自改进方法，作为一种agent后训练技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出以知识为中心的自改进方法，作为一种agent后训练技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出以知识为中心的自改进方法，作为一种agent后训练技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies knowledge-centric self-improvement where agents contribute to a curated knowledge base, enabling persistent improvement across tasks without agent-specific optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对agent自改进通常绑定特定设计难迁移的问题，提出知识中心范式：通用agent完成任务后向共享知识库贡献证据驱动的见解，经整理后新agent可高效利用。实验展示该协议能持续积累跨任务知识，为agent后训练提供了一条可迁移、低成本的改进路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对agent自改进通常绑定特定设计难迁移的问题，提出知识中心范式：通用agent完成任务后向共享知识库贡献证据驱动的见解，经整理后新agent可高效利用。实验展示该协议能持续积累跨任务知识，为agent后训练提供了一条可迁移、低成本的改进路径。
